revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33Die Verwendung der Hieroglyphe T13 statt T14 widerspricht der üblichen Schreibung. Im Originaltext ist T13 zusätzlich gespiegelt und um 90° gedreht zu sehen. Ursache ist die teilweise [[Altägyptische Kryptographie|kryptographische Schreibung]] des Nutbuches, vgl. Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, S. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''{{unicode|Tm3.t-ẖr.t}}''
''Unterer der Matte''}} '''Temat-cheret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der vier [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen Sternbild [[Temat-heret-cheret|Matte]] gehörte. == Hintergrund == [[Bild:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Kreuz des Südens]] Das auffälligste [[Himmelsobjekt]] ist hierbei der untere [[Stern|Hauptstern]] [[Acrux]] vom [[Sternbild]] [[Kreuz des Südens]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] war dieser Dekan gemeinsam mit [[Temat-heret]] zu dem Gesamtdekan [[Temat-heret-cheret]] am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] als fünfter Dekan zusammengefasst. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 85–86. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. == Einzelnachweise == [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Sternbild (Altägypten)]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = <hiero>t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33</hiero><ref>The use of the hieroglyph <hiero>T13</hiero> instead of <hiero>T14</hiero> deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, <hiero>T13</hiero> is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:New pages]][[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret

''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Temat-cheret]] [[de:Temat-cheret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:new pages]] [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Temat-cheret-qq]] [[en:draft:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Temat-cheret-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Temat-cheret-qq]] [[en:draft:Temat-cheret]] [[de:Temat-cheret]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Temat-cheret-qq]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Temat-cheret]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Temat-cheret]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Temat-cheret-qq]] [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Temat-cheret-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Temat-cheret]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|qq=draft}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Temat-cheret]] [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[sn:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] [[sn:emat-cheret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-cheret]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-cheret]][[Category:qq]]

''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|lt=Ancient Egyptian|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft}} is the lower {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} [[Acrux]] of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Temat-cheret]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Temat-cheret]] [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] [[sn:emat-cheret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]]

In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret

''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Temat-cheret]] }} [[sn:Temat-cheret]] [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] [[sn:emat-cheret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q2402466]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=}} of the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Temat-cheret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Temat-cheret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Temat-cheret]] }} [[sn:Temat-cheret]] [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] [[sn:emat-cheret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Temat-cheret]][[Category:qq]]

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.text:
[[de:Temat-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2402466]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402466}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402466}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[de:Temat-cheret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q2402466]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2402466}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2402466}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egyptian]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the lower [[:en:Star|Star]] [[:en:Acrux|Acrux]] of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret
''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the lower {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=Constellation}} [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402466}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''Tm3.t-ẖr.t''
''Lower of the Mat''}} '''Temat-cheret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471|1=Temat-heret-cheret}} (Mat). == Background == [[File:Southern cross2.jpg|thumb|Southern Cross]] The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999|1=Astronomical object}} is the lower {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} {{ill|Acrux|en|Acrux|WD=Q13102|lt=Acrux|1=Acrux}} of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=Constellation|1=Constellation}} [[:en:Southern Cross|Southern Cross]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, this decan, together with {{ill|Temat-heret|de|Temat-heret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret|qq=|WD=Q1549527|1=Temat-heret}}, was combined into the overall decan {{ill|Temat-heret-cheret|de|Temat-heret-cheret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temat-heret-cheret|qq=|WD=Q2402471|1=Temat-heret-cheret}} on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}} as the fifth decan. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Temat-cheret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Temat-cheret]] [[ja:テマト・チェレト]] {{wikidata link|Q2402466}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = t:T13-W12-D21:t N14-N33*N33*N33:N33The use of the hieroglyph T13 instead of T14 deviates from the usual spelling. In the original text, T13 is additionally mirrored and rotated 90°. The cause is the partially {{ill|Ancient Egyptian cryptography|de|Altägyptische Kryptographie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian cryptography|qq=|WD=Q447064|1=Ancient Egyptian cryptography}} of the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut|1=Book of Nut}}, see Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch'', The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 385. |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Temat-cheret

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 85–86. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''.
